metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm ADRs 0026-0029 (bridge convergence governing spec) are committed to `docs/adr/` on the working branch before implementation begins; the current `docs/adr/0026-autodeploy-on-box-timer.md` is unrelated and the bridge-convergence ADRs 0027-0029 are absent entirely — without them executors and reviewers have no primary source for the direction-arbitration decision points, the four-way ObservedJira model, or the level-trigger contract.
findings.finding: ADRs 0026-0029 are referenced as the governing spec for all invariants (direction arbitration, 404 observation model, echo suppression, level-trigger contract), but only ADR 0026 exists in docs/adr/ and it covers 'continuous auto-deploy on systemd timer' — entirely unrelated to bridge convergence. ADRs 0027-0029 are absent from the repo.
findings.location: Notes section ('invariants this work must preserve are documented in ADRs 0026-0029'); baf7 Implementation Contract ('All invariants are specified by ADRs 0026-0029 (docs/adr/) — read them FIRST'); AC lines referencing 'ADR 0026 #1-#3', 'ADR 0028 regression cells', 'ADR 0029 #3'
findings.suggested_fix: Create and commit docs/adr/0027-bridge-level-trigger-contract.md, docs/adr/0028-jira-observation-model.md, docs/adr/0029-echo-suppression-and-snapshot-contract.md (renaming/renumbering as appropriate so ADR 0026 for bridge convergence gets the next free slot after 0026-autodeploy). The baf7 implementation contract already contains the four decision points inline — elevate that prose into the ADR files so the classifier can be built and verified against a stable, citable spec.

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a per-binding quarantine mechanism (quarantine_count field in bindings.json, entry/exit policy, reporting in census and bridge-fsck binding_drift output) to baf7's BindingStore spec and allocate ownership to exactly one child.
findings.finding: Per-binding quarantine (named explicitly in parent AC6 as a required safety rail) is not implemented or owned by any child. baf7 implements the circuit breaker and census but no quarantine mechanism; 5854 references L11 double-bind quarantine only in the specific context of label-collision, not as a general per-binding quarantine. No child defines quarantine entry/exit logic, quarantine storage, or per-binding quarantine state in bindings.json.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a quarantine_count: int field alongside baseline in the bindings.json entry (baf7 baseline schema extension). In classify.py, add a QUARANTINED decision kind (non-acting, non-NOOP) for bindings whose apply has failed >= threshold consecutive times. The live pass skips QUARANTINED bindings (no mutation); bridge-fsck/8de5 reports them in binding_drift. Exit quarantine on a successful apply (clear quarantine_count). Assign ownership to baf7 (schema + Decision kind) with application in the apply path (7d23 Phase 4 or a guardrail in each drift child).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `bindings.json` schema rollback procedure (e.g., specify that extra fields are forward-only and old code must tolerate unknown keys, or provide a migration script to strip baseline fields) so a code rollback after shadow-phase writes is safe and documented.
findings.finding: The plan names the `workflow_dispatch` mode input as the rollback brake but does not address backward-compatibility of `bindings.json` after per-binding baseline fields have been written. If old code is re-deployed (rollback), it will encounter an evolved `bindings.json` schema; the plan does not specify whether extra fields are silently ignored, cause parse errors, or require a migration step to strip them.
findings.location: AC item 10 (BASELINE DUAL-WRITE SHADOW) / Rollout section (soar-quill-flare/7d23)
findings.suggested_fix: State explicitly that the baseline fields are additive and old code MUST treat unknown `bindings.json` keys as ignorable (tolerant reader pattern); add a one-liner migration script to strip baseline fields if needed, and reference it from the rollout child's back-out section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the concrete value of N (minimum live passes before prev_snapshot consumer swap) in the BASELINE DUAL-WRITE SHADOW AC — e.g. 'N=5 consecutive clean passes' — so the criterion is measurable and unambiguous.
findings.finding: The baseline dual-write shadow AC specifies 'N live passes' as the promotion threshold, but N is never defined anywhere in the plan, leaving an implementing agent unable to determine when the prev_snapshot consumer swap is permitted.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — BASELINE DUAL-WRITE SHADOW bullet: 'persisted alongside prev_snapshot for >= N live passes WITHOUT being consumed'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '>=N live passes' with a specific threshold (e.g. '>=5 consecutive clean passes with zero divergence alerts') and store that constant in a named config key so CI can assert it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Promote the circuit-breaker threshold from the derisk notes into the Safety rails AC as an explicit constant (e.g. 'N=10%'), making the criterion self-contained and verifiable.
findings.finding: The circuit-breaker acceptance criterion states 'mutating/retiring > N% of bindings' without defining N in the AC text; the value is only implied (10%) in the derisk evidence notes, which an implementing agent may not be expected to treat as normative.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Safety rails bullet: 'circuit breaker refuses a pass mutating/retiring > N% of bindings'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC bullet to read: 'circuit breaker refuses a pass mutating/retiring > 10% of bindings (CIRCUIT_BREAKER_THRESHOLD = 0.10)' and reference the same constant in the implementation contract.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'probe' and 'probe-budget' in the plan (e.g. in a Glossary or the OBSERVABILITY AC): what operation constitutes a probe, the budget limit per pass, and how probe-budget usage is computed and emitted.
findings.finding: 'probe-budget usage' is listed as a required field in the OBSERVABILITY census record but the concept of a 'probe' and its budget is never defined anywhere in the plan, leaving an agent unable to implement or test this metric.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — OBSERVABILITY bullet: 'probe-budget usage' as a census field

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the binding/issue count across sections: clarify whether '614 bindings / 614 issues' vs '613 bound+present keys' means 1 binding targets a deleted issue (a class-C instance in the frozen artifact), and update both the AC and the Derisk Evidence section to state the same numbers with an explicit note on any delta.
findings.finding: The FROZEN-ARTIFACT CENSUS REPLAY TEST AC states '614 bindings / 614 issues' as the frozen production artifact size, but the Derisk Evidence section says 'all 5 mirrored fields present for all 613 bound+present keys' — a one-unit discrepancy that will ambiguate the exact expected counts when the implementer writes the regression test assertion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — FROZEN-ARTIFACT CENSUS REPLAY TEST item vs Derisk evidence section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical to the FROZEN-ARTIFACT CENSUS REPLAY TEST AC: '614 bindings total; 613 bound-and-present, 1 bound-to-deleted-issue (class-C instance in artifact)' and mirror that breakdown in the Derisk Evidence section so the implementer can code the assertion without offline investigation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the child ticket for D (hale-cameo-basin/8de5) and the OBSERVABILITY AC a requirement to document the updated `bridge-fsck` exit-code semantics and the DECISION CENSUS log record schema (field names, types, and example) in a runbook or the relevant ADR.
findings.finding: The behavioral change to `bridge-fsck` (from exit-0 / 'No issues found' on binding-level drift to actively reporting it) and the new structured DECISION CENSUS log format are new observable contracts for operators, but the plan does not mention updating any runbook, operator doc, or `bridge-fsck` usage reference.
findings.location: AC item 5 (bridge-fsck consumer) / AC item 9 (OBSERVABILITY / DECISION CENSUS) / drift class D (hale-cameo-basin/8de5)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Designate the circuit-breaker mutation threshold (N%) and the shadow-phase pass count (N) as explicitly configurable parameters (e.g., env var or config entry), not hardcoded constants, so they can be tuned without a code change.
findings.finding: The circuit-breaker threshold (N% of bindings) and shadow-pass count (N clean passes before consumer swap) are referenced as placeholders throughout the plan but are never explicitly designated as configurable parameters (env var / config entry). These thresholds are expected to change as binding-store size grows and operational confidence increases, yet the plan does not commit to making them configurable vs. hardcoding them.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'circuit breaker refuses a pass mutating/retiring > N% of bindings'; 'persisted alongside prev_snapshot for >= N live passes'
findings.suggested_fix: In the child ticket baf7 (FOUNDATION), define `RECONCILE_BREAKER_PCT` (default 10) and `RECONCILE_SHADOW_PASSES` (default e.g. 5) as environment-variable-backed config values read at reconciler startup, logged in the DECISION CENSUS record each pass.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify and test a post-consumer-swap rollback procedure (e.g., keep prev_snapshot code path behind a feature flag for at least one additional release cycle, or document the exact steps and SLO to restore it), and add a rollback-verification step to child 7d23's guardrails.
findings.finding: After the prev_snapshot consumer is swapped out at the end of child 7d23 (soar-quill-flare), the plan does not specify or test an explicit rollback path — the 'workflow_dispatch mode input is the rollback brake' only covers mode-ladder reversion during the shadow phase, not post-swap recovery if issues emerge once prev_snapshot logic has been removed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'ROLLOUT behind the mode ladder' bullet and 'BASELINE DUAL-WRITE SHADOW' bullet; child soar-quill-flare/7d23
findings.suggested_fix: In child 7d23's implementation contract, retain the prev_snapshot code path behind a `RECONCILE_BASELINE_MODE=legacy|new` env flag (not deleted) for one release cycle after the swap; add a rollback AC that asserts switching the flag back restores prior behavior without data cleanup.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring a parametrized regression test that encodes each of L1-L23 as a named scenario (inputs + expected Decision/action), so preservation of every lesson is an explicit, runnable assertion rather than an implicit claim.
findings.finding: The Success Criteria states 'Every existing lesson L1-L23 is preserved (no regression)' but no AC specifies a test that exercises each of the 23 lesson scenarios individually — the observable proof is indirect (frozen artifact replay covers only 7 known decisions; randomized property tests cover convergence but not lesson-specific edge cases).
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'Every existing lesson L1-L23 is preserved' bullet; Acceptance Criteria — convergence property test and FROZEN-ARTIFACT CENSUS REPLAY TEST bullets
findings.suggested_fix: In child baf7's scope, create `tests/test_classifier_lessons.py` with one `@pytest.mark.parametrize` entry per lesson (L1–L23), each with a comment citing the lesson number and the original commit/PR that introduced it, asserting the expected Decision output.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In baf7's Implementation contract, add one sentence specifying the canonical call sequence: 'For every CONFIRMED_404 observation, the consumer calls note_absent(jira_key) exactly once regardless of the Decision returned; note_absent() is self-contained (increments + conditionally retires); the consumer MUST NOT call retire() separately after a RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE decision.'
findings.finding: The call contract between classify()'s RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE / PROBE_GET decisions and the note_absent() enactor is underspecified: the plan says 'classify predicts, note_absent enacts — no double count' but does not state whether the live consumer calls note_absent() for every CONFIRMED_404 observation (regardless of predicted decision), or only when classify returns PROBE_GET and not RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE. If a consumer calls note_absent() after classify already returned RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE, the note_absent enactor increments the count AND internally fires retirement — which is correct — but if a consumer skips note_absent() when classify returns RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE and directly retires, it bypasses the reversible soft-delete machinery and the alert in _retire(). Each child ticket (13eb, 444d) consumes this seam; without an explicit call contract the two implementers may wire it differently.
findings.location: baf7 Implementation contract — 'Grace-met for RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE' section; child tickets 444d and 13eb Implementation contracts
findings.suggested_fix: Append to baf7 baf7 Implementation contract grace section: 'Canonical consumer call sequence for a CONFIRMED_404 observation: (1) call note_absent(jira_key) — this is the sole enactor, idempotent if the entry was already retired; (2) read the Decision from classify() to determine logging/reporting only. Never call _retire() or unbind() directly in a consumer; never skip note_absent() when classify returns RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In 8de5's Implementation contract, specify the canonical ObservedJira value the offline bridge-fsck arm injects when no Jira snapshot is available (e.g. 'inject ObservedJira.PRESENT for all bound+locally-terminal pairs to report would-be TERMINAL_TRANSITION; use a dedicated UNKNOWN sentinel or document that offline results are conservative'), and explain what that assumption implies for reporting accuracy.
findings.finding: The offline arm of bridge-fsck (drift class D / ticket 8de5) calls classify() with an assumed ObservedJira state rather than a real observation, but the plan does not specify what ObservedJira value is injected for a bound key whose Jira side is unknown offline. The correct cell for 'bound + local archived + Jira unknown' requires injecting ABSENT_IN_WINDOW (conservative) or PRESENT (optimistic), and the choice materially changes the reported Decision: ABSENT_IN_WINDOW → PROBE_GET, PRESENT → TERMINAL_TRANSITION. If the implementer injects CONFIRMED_404, the offline arm would incorrectly report would-be RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE for every archived-bound ticket, even live ones.
findings.location: 8de5 Implementation contract §1 (Offline arm); epic AC: 'One classifier, TWO consumers: the live pass acts; bridge-fsck (offline, reads bindings.json)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify exactly when and how `seed_baselines_from_snapshot(prev_snapshot)` is invoked at the start of Phase 1 (e.g. a one-time bootstrap step in the rollout runbook, or auto-triggered on the first pass where `baseline_dual_write=true` is set and no stored baselines exist) so the shadow_check comparison starts from a populated baseline rather than flooding alerts for all ~613 pre-existing bindings.
findings.finding: The rollout plan (7d23 Phase 1) enables baseline dual-write shadowing but never specifies when or how `seed_baselines_from_snapshot` is invoked to pre-populate baselines for the ~613 already-bound keys before the shadow comparison begins. Without pre-seeding, the very first dual-write pass will have no stored baselines to compare against (all absent), the shadow_check will log divergence for every key (M=613), bridge_alerts will flood, and the '10 consecutive clean passes (M==0)' gate — the sole safety mechanism before Phase 3's consumer swap — becomes unachievable until all 613 keys happen to have been re-written by at least one live pass.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — BASELINE DUAL-WRITE SHADOW bullet; child ticket soar-quill-flare/7d23 Phase 1
findings.suggested_fix: In 7d23 Phase 1, add an explicit bootstrap step: on the first pass where `reconciler.baseline_dual_write=true` and `BindingStore.get_baseline(local_id) is None` for any bound key, call `seed_baselines_from_snapshot(prev_snapshot)` before the shadow comparison loop. This is idempotent (per the baf7 spec) and ensures the shadow_check starts comparing against meaningful values rather than absent ones.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile parent AC11 wording: replace 'asserts EXACTLY the 7 known acting decisions' with 'asserts EXACTLY the 7 known non-NOOP decisions (5x TERMINAL_TRANSITION, 1x PROBE_GET, 1x ADOPT) and census.acting_count == 6 (PROBE_GET is non-acting per circuit breaker)'.
findings.finding: Parent AC11 states 'asserts EXACTLY the 7 known acting decisions' but baf7's implementation contract explicitly states acting_count == 6 (PROBE_GET is not in the acting set for circuit breaker purposes), creating a terminology conflict that will cause the frozen-census test to fail if written literally against the parent AC.
findings.location: parent AC11 vs child baf7 (baf7-868d-bb7e-4539) implementation contract section 'Acting kinds vs the 7 drift frozen census'
findings.suggested_fix: Update parent AC11 to read: 'asserts EXACTLY the 7 known non-NOOP decisions (5x TERMINAL_TRANSITION REB-464/456/465/466/457, 1x PROBE_GET REB-530, 1x ADOPT REB-532) and nothing else, AND that census(decisions).acting_count == 6 (PROBE_GET is a bounded read, not circuit-breaker-acting).' No change needed in baf7 — its implementation contract is already correct.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `is_retired(jira_key) -> bool` to baf7's BindingStore spec (handling missing bindings-retired.json as empty-set), and update 5854's dependency declaration to include 13eb OR move is_retired() ownership to baf7 and document that bindings-retired.json may not exist yet (return False when absent).
findings.finding: Child 5854 (ADOPT handler) requires `BindingStore.is_retired(jira_key)` as its first adoption gate (guardrails and implementation contract step 1), but this method and the bindings-retired.json file it reads are first created by child 13eb's GC retirement path. The parent plan states the four drift-class heals may be executed 'in any order', and 5854 only declares 'DEPENDS ON amiss-bun-wince (baf7)' — no dependency on 13eb is declared. Implementing 5854 before 13eb leaves is_retired() either absent or returning incorrect results.
findings.location: child 5854 (5854-b8d4-17be-4bb2) implementation contract step 1 and guardrails; child 13eb (13eb-c414-0763-4fa1) implementation contract live acceptance note; parent AC7 'four drift-class heals in any order'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `is_retired(jira_key: str) -> bool` to baf7's BindingStore (reads bindings-retired.json if present, returns False if absent — missing file = empty retired set). This makes is_retired() available from the foundation ticket, removes the hidden 5854→13eb dependency, and preserves the 'any order' guarantee. 13eb's first retirement write then populates the file that is_retired() already knows how to read. Update baf7 AC bullet 2 to include: 'is_retired(jira_key) -> bool on BindingStore; returns False when bindings-retired.json does not exist.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Designate one child (recommend 444d as class-A is the anchor drift class) as the owner of the shared binding-store loop scaffold in the apply path, and update 13eb's implementation contract to state 'extend the loop scaffolded by 444d' with the specific file and function name; or move the loop scaffold into baf7 as a utility.
findings.finding: Both 13eb and 444d claim ownership of the same binding-store loop ('unified with class A/C: one binding-store loop'), but neither child specifies which ticket scaffolds the shared loop and which adds cells into an existing loop. Since the parent allows these to be implemented in any order, concurrent or sequential implementation of the two children will either produce duplicate loop implementations (scope overlap) or leave one child waiting on an undefined interface from the other.
findings.location: child 13eb (13eb-c414-0763-4fa1) implementation contract: 'implement them against one shared iteration, not two loops'; child 444d (444d-c21f-2c82-4622) guardrails: 'Unify with class C (13eb): one binding-store loop heals terminal transitions and GCs dead bindings'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single sentence to baf7's implementation contract: 'The binding-store walk (for_each_binding iterator in reconcile.py or outbound_differ.py) is scaffolded by the foundation ticket and stubbed with a NOOP handler; 444d and 13eb each register their Decision cells into this shared walk.' Alternatively, designate 444d as the walk owner and add to 13eb: 'EXTEND the binding-store loop scaffolded by 444d (class A); do not create a second loop.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
